title: TLS downgrade / legacy-cipher handshake spike
exclusions: Does NOT measure certificate-expiry posture (covered by 22.41.2); focuses on observed handshake protocol/cipher.
evidence: Saved search 'tls_downgrade_spike' (hourly) emits a per-endpoint handshake profile and alerts on legacy-cipher spikes.

compliance.assurance_rationale: PCI 4.2.1 strong cryptography in transmission is directly measured.
Transmission security is required for ePHI.
SC-8 transmission confidentiality; legacy cipher negotiation is a direct failure.

controlTest.positiveScenario: An external load-balancer begins negotiating TLS 1.0 with 5% of clients after a config push; the UC fires within the hour.
controlTest.negativeScenario: All handshakes remain TLS 1.2/1.3 with strong ciphers; no alert.

dataSources: F5/NGINX/HAProxy TLS handshake logs, cloud-provider LB telemetry, TLS scanner longitudinal results.
spl:
index=tls sourcetype=f5:tls OR sourcetype=nginx:tls OR sourcetype=haproxy:tls earliest=-1h
| eval legacy=if(match(protocol,"TLSv1\.(0|1)") OR match(cipher,"3DES|RC4|NULL|DES|EXPORT"),1,0)
| stats sum(legacy) as legacy_hs count as total_hs by host
| eval legacy_pct=round(legacy_hs/total_hs*100,2)
| where legacy_pct>0.5 AND total_hs>100
| table host legacy_hs total_hs legacy_pct
description: Monitors observed TLS handshake metadata for any endpoint and alerts when legacy-cipher or legacy-protocol negotiations exceed a small threshold. Downgrade is a common attacker technique against partially-upgraded infrastructure.
value: Catches silent downgrade issues introduced by load-balancer config drift before they become an audit finding or data-exposure incident.
grandmaExplanation: Catches silent downgrade issues introduced by load-balancer config drift before they become an audit finding or data-exposure incident — so you can show exactly what is being watched and when something went wrong.
implementation: (1) Ensure LBs emit handshake telemetry; (2) schedule hourly; (3) route hits to security ops; (4) block via config-as-code rollback.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: F5/NGINX/HAProxy TLS handshake logs, cloud-provider LB telemetry, TLS scanner longitudinal results..
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Ensure LBs emit handshake telemetry; (2) schedule hourly; (3) route hits to security ops; (4) block via config-as-code rollback.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=tls sourcetype=f5:tls OR sourcetype=nginx:tls OR sourcetype=haproxy:tls earliest=-1h
| eval legacy=if(match(protocol,"TLSv1\.(0|1)") OR match(cipher,"3DES|RC4|NULL|DES|EXPORT"),1,0)
| stats sum(legacy) as legacy_hs count as total_hs by host
| eval legacy_pct=round(legacy_hs/total_hs*100,2)
| where legacy_pct>0.5 AND total_hs>100
| table host legacy_hs total_hs legacy_pct
```

Understanding this SPL

**TLS downgrade / legacy-cipher handshake spike** — Catches silent downgrade issues introduced by load-balancer config drift before they become an audit finding or data-exposure incident.

Documented **Data sources**: F5/NGINX/HAProxy TLS handshake logs, cloud-provider LB telemetry, TLS scanner longitudinal results. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: tls; **sourcetype**: f5:tls, nginx:tls, haproxy:tls. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=tls, sourcetype=f5:tls, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **legacy** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by host** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
• `eval` defines or adjusts **legacy_pct** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where legacy_pct>0.5 AND total_hs>100` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Pipeline stage (see **TLS downgrade / legacy-cipher handshake spike**): table host legacy_hs total_hs legacy_pct


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Line chart of legacy-handshake percentage per endpoint, table of offending endpoints, single-value '% handshakes on strong TLS (24h)'.
visualization: Line chart of legacy-handshake percentage per endpoint, table of offending endpoints, single-value '% handshakes on strong TLS (24h)'.

references.title: MITRE ATT&CK — T1562.006 Impair Defenses: Indicator Blocking

knownFalsePositives: Legacy client populations (POS devices) can inflate TLS 1.0 usage in scope of a documented risk-acceptance; allow-list such endpoints.
